title: hieracium bifidum subsp. adlasiocybe
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. adlasiocybe, also known as Adlasi's hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its unique, leaf-like stems and small yellow flowers. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hieracium bifidum subsp. adlasiocybe include its ability to promote healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against infection. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally to aid in recovery.
